title: Lipid Analysis Assay by GC-Mass Spectrometry
author: Henri Brunengraber
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yehftb6
materials: Homogenize on ice with polytron homogenizer (tissue only)
Cover and heat for 3 hours at 85ºC

steps.title: Pipette 1ml KOH ethanol solution (1N...
steps.instruction: Pipette 1ml KOH ethanol solution (1N KOH in 70% EtOH) for every 100mg of tissue or 100 μl of plasma use glass screw top tubes (may use less tissue/plasma)

steps.title: Internals standards (IS)
steps.instruction: Internals standards (IS): add 25 μl of 1mg/ml heptadecanoic acid (C17:0) and cholesterol-d7 for every 100μl of tissue (note: adjust added amount of internal standards by testing a representative sample)

steps.title: Pipette 50-100μl of solution into an Eppendorf tube
steps.instruction: Pipette 50-100μl of solution into an Eppendorf tube

steps.title: Take 200μl of chloroform phase (bottom...
steps.instruction: Take 200μl of chloroform phase (bottom layer) and dry in GC vial at 75ºC

steps.title: React with 60μl of TMS, cover,...
steps.instruction: React with 60μl of TMS, cover, heat at 75ºC for 20 minutes

steps.title: Transfer to GC insert and cap GC-MS Analysis
steps.instruction: Transfer to GC insert and cap GC-MS Analysis: Lipid TMS derivatives are analyzed using an Agilent 5973N-MSD equipped with an Agilent 6890 GC system, and a DB-17MS capillary column (30 m x 0.25 mm x 0.25 um). The mass spectrometer is operated in the electron impact mode (EI; 70 eV).

steps.title: Selective ion monitoring of mass-to-charge ratios (m/z) a
steps.instruction: Selective ion monitoring of mass-to-charge ratios (m/z) a. Palmitate: M0=313-M+; IS, C:17=327 b. Cholesterol: M0=368-M+; IS, Cholesterol-d 7 =375 c. For other lipids: see references
